title: Large Ceramic Planter with Baroque Fruit Decor
description:
We call this a cachepot — a large decorative planter, 40 cm across. It's a serious piece of ceramics. The body is a rounded pot shape with a wide mouth that narrows slightly at the base, glazed in a deep emerald green with a sponged texture. Around the belly, our artisans have painted elaborate baroque scrollwork in golden yellow, framing hand-painted fruits — we can see a pomegranate split open on one side and a bunch of blue grapes on the other, all surrounded by curling acanthus leaves.

A yellow decorative band runs along the top rim with a palmette pattern, and another yellow band sits near the base. The green is rich and layered, with the sponging giving it depth rather than a flat finish. The golden scrollwork is painted with confident, sweeping brushstrokes — the kind of detail that takes years of practice to get right.

The cachepot works indoors or outdoors in a covered area. Most customers use it for a large plant or small tree, but it also looks good on its own as a decorative floor piece. The 40 cm size makes it substantial enough to anchor a corner of a room or a terrace. We also have this in 30 cm and 35 cm sizes — just send us a request if you'd prefer a different size.

Made by hand in Vietri sul Mare as part of our Barocco Collection. Each cachepot is individually shaped and painted by our master ceramists, so the scrollwork and fruit details will vary slightly from piece to piece.
excerpt: A 40 cm ceramic cachepot in emerald green with baroque scrollwork and hand-painted fruit. Made by artisans in Vietri sul Mare.
seoTitle: Large Ceramic Planter 40 cm — Ceramiche Da Mario
seoDescription: Handmade 40 cm ceramic cachepot in emerald green with baroque fruit decor, painted by artisans in Vietri sul Mare. Ceramiche Da Mario, Praiano.
